rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 138-139 contain dated session history, but they fall below maintainer-cut (line 80); dropped per procedural rule

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 87: 'Plug Points (trainer):' is in body but is a maintainer-labeled section. However, the term appears in a section heading (canonical module-shape element, exempt per check_student_facing rule 2 carve-out)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: 38: 'The scoreboard IS the explanation.' — positions scoreboard as defense against vendor narrative; reads defensive. 41: 'Magic that you can point at' — defensive against 'black-box' vendor critique. These import positioning into teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.fix: Reframe Key Concepts as emergent learning, not defensive positioning. Lead with what the student understands: 'Benchmarking teaches you how to evaluate evaluators—a move you'll use for every future quality question.' Drop the scoreboard-as-proof framing; keep the discipline and method.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Author-"we" ban — first-person-plural extension
rules_evaluated.evidence: 45: 'Today we measure what the system actually says inside its scope.'
rules_evaluated.fix: Recast as second-person or module-as-subject: 'In this module, measure what the system actually says inside its scope.' Or: 'The module measures what the system actually says.' Removes author-we.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: 9: 'Agent-sprawl reading from Module 4 covered shadow agents' — terms appear without earning. 28: 'four detectors' appear before explaining what detection is. 36: 'Benchmarking as a pattern' — jargon before operational context.
rules_evaluated.fix: Earn jargon on first use: 'Module 4 reading covered how multiple uncoordinated agents (agent-sprawl) create visibility gaps. Now you measure which detection method works for your output.' Or move jargon-heavy summary to after Key Concepts when the student has done the work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap
Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: 16: 'synthesized judge file' appears without one-breath primer. 21: 'judge' appears 2x before earning. 40: 'judge file' at Key Concepts line 40 — still unearned. Line 40 is in body above fence.
rules_evaluated.fix: On line 15 or 16, add primer before first use of 'judge': 'and save the winning detector method as a reusable file — a judge that names what it catches and what it can't reach.' Then the term is earned for Key Concepts and Prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default)
No narrating the student's past or present state
rules_evaluated.evidence: 23: 'Your Module 4 residuals stay named, not solved' — presumes the student has Module 4 residuals and is aware of them. Patronizing narration of assumed student state. 22: 'You'd stake your reputation on some of it' — presumes psychological state.
rules_evaluated.fix: Recast as module-state or artifact-state, not student-state: 'Module 3's claims vary in strength. Module 4's residuals stay named, not solved — that's orthogonal to groundedness.' Removes presumption about student's awareness or psychology.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences
Inline links use the target's title, never its filename
No placeholders inside fenced blocks
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 48 ('Prompt' label) precedes the fenced block at line 50, but the semantic lead-in (lines 47-49) is buried in narrative prose. Should be: one short sentence with command verb BEFORE the **Prompt** label.
rules_evaluated.fix: Add explicit one-sentence lead-in before line 48: 'Ask Claude to review the session and update `./CLAUDE.md` with groundedness operating rules.' Then blank line, then **Prompt** label. Current structure buries the semantic intent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, never by invented alias
Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: 3-42: Big Idea and body do not explicitly name the emotional endpoint. Mood ('mechanical rescue, not triumph') lives only in maintainer block (lines 112-116), not visible to student.
rules_evaluated.fix: Body must name what state the student leaves in. Add to Big Idea or opening: 'You walk out with the first judge you can defend — not magic, just measured' or similar. Mood lives in student-visible prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
